source: Authentic narrations from the Six Canonical Books (Kutub as-Sittah)

hadiths.text: The best among you are those who have the best manners and character.
None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
Actions are judged by intentions, and every person will have what they intended.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should speak good or remain silent.
The strong is not the one who overcomes others by his strength, but the one who controls himself when angry.
A Muslim is one from whose tongue and hand other Muslims are safe.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult. Give glad tidings and do not repel.
Religion is sincerity — to Allah, His Book, His Messenger, and to the leaders and common folk of the Muslims.
He is not one of us who does not show mercy to our young and respect to our elders.
Cleanliness is half of faith.
The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if they are small.
A kind word is charity.
Smiling at your brother is charity.
Whoever does not show mercy will not be shown mercy.
The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever.
Modesty brings nothing but good.
Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all matters.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honour his guest.
The supplication of a Muslim for his brother in his absence is answered.
Whoever guides another to good will have a reward like the one who does it.
Two blessings which many people waste: good health and free time.
Whoever among you sees evil, let him change it with his hand; if not, then with his tongue; and if not, then with his heart — and that is the weakest of faith.
The most perfect of believers in faith are those with the best character.
Allah does not look at your appearance or wealth, but rather He looks at your hearts and deeds.
A person is upon the religion of his close friend, so let each of you look to whom he befriends.
Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveller.
The most beloved places to Allah are the mosques, and the most disliked places to Allah are the markets.
He who does not thank people does not thank Allah.
Truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.
Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the most untruthful of speech.
The believers in their mutual love and mercy are like one body — when one limb suffers, the whole body responds with fever and sleeplessness.
Whoever relieves a believer's distress in this world, Allah will relieve his distress on the Day of Resurrection.
The best of you is the one who is best to his family, and I am the best of you to my family.
Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
Whoever treads a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.
Remember often the destroyer of pleasures: death.
The upper hand is better than the lower hand — the upper hand is the one that gives, and the lower hand is the one that receives.
Allah is more pleased with the repentance of His servant than one of you who finds his lost camel after losing it.
The supplication of a parent for his child is not rejected.
The best charity is that given while you are healthy, fearful of poverty, and hoping for wealth.
Whoever fasts Ramadan out of faith and hoping for reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.
When a person dies, his deeds end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who prays for him.
Patience is at the first stroke of calamity.
Whoever loves to meet Allah, Allah loves to meet him.
The most beloved words to Allah are four: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illa Allah, and Allahu Akbar.
Whoever stays awake the Night of Decree out of faith and hoping for reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.
The example of a believer who recites the Qur'an is that of a citron — its fragrance is pleasant and its taste is sweet.
Whoever takes a path in search of knowledge, Allah eases for him a path to Paradise.
Pray as you have seen me pray.
Verily, Allah is beautiful and loves beauty.
